# callback.

> A local-first app that tailors your résumé — and, optionally, a cover letter — to a
> specific job, **grounded in what's actually true about you, not invented**. It reads
> a job posting, asks a few sharp questions, helps you pick which of your real
> accomplishments fit, writes a grounded tailored draft, and renders it to Word or PDF.
> It runs on your own machine; your career data never leaves it apart from the AI calls
> that do the writing.

The committed knowledge wiki under `docs/wiki/` is the best map of the system — start
there. The wiki is a synthesized layer over the repo at git HEAD; on any conflict the
canonical docs below win.

## Wiki

- [Wiki index](docs/wiki/index.md): one-line summary of every wiki page
- [System overview](docs/wiki/overview.md): what callback. is and how the system is shaped
- [Wiki SCHEMA](docs/wiki/SCHEMA.md): wiki conventions, the grounding rule, the source model

## Core docs

- [System model](docs/system-model.md): the canonical seven-functions self-model + the one-way dependency law
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): module map + the deterministic / LLM-routing boundary
- [Product shape](docs/PRODUCT_SHAPE.md): the corpus-item model + the v1 → v2 ladder
- [Vision](vision.md): product intent and the constraints callback. holds itself to
- [README](README.md): user-facing overview

## Contributor contract

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): the universal AI-agent contract — security gate, deterministic/LLM boundary, conventions
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Claude-Code-specific overrides, hooks, and the skill catalog
- [CONTRIBUTING](CONTRIBUTING.md): branch + commit conventions and the dev loop
- [SECURITY](SECURITY.md): threat model and API-key rules
